query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Logic', 'The principles governing correct or reliable inference', 'Her logic became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Logical', 'In agreement with the principles of logic', 'Her logical became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Logician', 'A person who studies or teaches logic', 'Her logician became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Logistics', 'The planning, implementation, and coordination of details', 'Scholars noted a striking logistics between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Loiterer', 'One who loiters, one who lingers or hangs around', 'Scholars noted a striking loiterer between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Loll', 'To act lazily or indolently, to recline, to lean', 'The author hoped the new policy would loll tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Longevity', 'The quality of being long-lasting, especially of life', 'Scholars noted a striking longevity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Loot', 'To steal, especially as part of war, riot', 'Critics argued that the plan might loot the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Loquacious', 'Very talkative', 'The passage presents a loquacious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Loquacity', 'Talkativeness', 'Scholars noted a striking loquacity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lordl', 'An unimportant or petty lord', 'Scholars noted a striking lordl between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lough', 'A lake or long, narrow inlet, especially in ireland', 'Scholars noted a striking lough between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Louse', 'To remove lice from the body of a person or animal', 'Over time, the community learned to louse its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lucid', 'Clear and easy to understand', '“Her explanation was so lucid that even a beginner could grasp the concept.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lucrative', 'Producing a surplus, profitable', 'The passage presents a lucrative portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lucubration', 'Laborious work, study, thought, etc , especially at night', 'The essay highlights the lucubration of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ludicrous', 'So foolish or unreasonable as to be amusing', 'The reviewer described the tone as ludicrous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lugubrious', 'Looking or sounding sad and dismal', 'The reviewer described the tone as lugubrious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lukewarm', 'Temperature between warm and cool', 'Scholars noted a striking lukewarm between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lumen', 'The amount of light that falls on a unit area', 'Her lumen became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Luminary', 'One that is an inspiration to others, who has had success', 'The author hoped the new policy would luminary tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Luminescence', 'The emission of light by a substance not resulting from heat', '“The jellyfish glowed with an eerie luminescence in the dark waters.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Luminous', 'Brightly shining', 'The reviewer described the tone as luminous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lummox', 'A clumsy, stupid person', 'Her lummox became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lunacy', 'The state of being mad, insanity', 'The essay highlights the lunacy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lunar', 'Of, or pertaining to, the moon', 'The essay highlights the lunar of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lunatic', 'An insane person', 'Scholars noted a striking lunatic between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lune', 'Anything shaped like a crescent or a half moon', 'Scholars noted a striking lune between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lurid', 'Very vivid in color or shocking in effect', 'What seemed lurid at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Luscious', 'Sweet and pleasant, delicious', 'The passage presents a luscious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lustrous', 'Having a glow, having luster, shining', 'The reviewer described the tone as lustrous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Luxuriate', 'To enjoy luxury', 'The author hoped the new policy would luxuriate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Luxurious', 'Enjoying pleasures of luxury', 'The reviewer described the tone as luxurious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lying', 'Characterized by luxury, ministering or conducive to luxury', 'What seemed lying at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lynch', 'To execute without a proper trial, especially by hanging', 'Critics argued that the plan might lynch the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lyre', 'A stringed musical instrument', 'The essay highlights the lyre of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Macabre', 'Representing or personifying death', 'Her macabre became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Macadamize', 'To cover with small, broken stones to form a smooth surface', 'Critics argued that the plan might macadamize the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Macerate', 'To soften or separate (something) into pieces by soaking', 'Critics argued that the plan might macerate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Machination', 'A clever scheme or artful plot, usually crafted for evil', 'Her machination became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Machinery', 'The parts of a machine, collectively', 'The essay highlights the machinery of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Machinist', 'One versed in the principles of machines', 'Her machinist became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Macrocosm', 'A complex structure, considered a single entity', 'The essay highlights the macrocosm of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Madden', 'To make angry', 'Over time, the community learned to madden its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Madrigal', 'A song for a small number of unaccompanied voices', 'Her madrigal became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Maelstrom', 'A powerful storm; turmoil', 'The essay highlights the maelstrom of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Magician', 'An entertainer skilled in creating illusions', 'Scholars noted a striking magician between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Magisterial', 'Befitting the status of a master, authoritative', 'The essay highlights the magisterial of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Magistracy', 'The office or dignity of a magistrate', 'The essay highlights the magistracy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Magnanimous', 'Generous, forgiving, or noble in spirit', '“Despite their rivalry, she was magnanimous in victory.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
